case_name: Sujeet Acharya M.D., Texas Oncology P.A., and Texas Urology Specialists v. Bernice Marie Gomez

summary.brief: Sujeet Acharya M.D., Texas Oncology P.A., and Texas Urology Specialists v. Bernice Marie Gomez. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Gomez was diagnosed with a malignant tumor in her left adrenal gland that was causing her abdominal pain.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Acharya performed surgery on Gomez on November 14, 2016, to remove the cancerous adrenal gland.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A post-surgical pathology report revealed that only benign tissue from Gomez’s pancreas had been removed, not the cancerous adrenal tissue.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The standard of care in treating an adrenal malignancy with adrenalectomy is absolutely clear: the adrenal gland containing the tumor should be removed.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Link

evidence_index.text: Dr. Link explained that Dr. Acharya breached the standard of care by failing to remove the adrenal gland.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Link

evidence_index.text: We affirm the trial court’s order denying appellants’ motion to dismiss.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Dr. Link’s report provides a fair summary of his opinions regarding the causal relationship between the breaches of the standard of care he identifies and the injuries to Gomez.
evidence_index.speaker: Court analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Gomez was diagnosed with a malignant tumor in her left adrenal gland that was causing her abdominal pain.

focused_exam.abdomen: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

critical_findings: Malignant tumor in left adrenal gland

requestables.name: Post-surgical pathology report
requestables.description: Pathology report of the tissue removed during surgery

reveal.result_summary: Only benign tissue from Gomez’s pancreas had been removed
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates failure to remove the cancerous adrenal tissue
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt re-evaluation of surgical approach

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with symptoms related to adrenal tumor

timeline_phases.name: Surgical Procedure
timeline_phases.description: Surgery to remove the cancerous adrenal gland

key_events.event: Pathology report reveals removal of benign pancreatic tissue

decision_points.clinical_context: During surgery to remove the cancerous adrenal gland
decision_points.prompt: What should the surgeon ensure during the adrenalectomy?

options.description: Ensure correct identification and removal of the adrenal gland
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care requires removal of the adrenal gland containing the tumor

options.description: Proceed with removal of tissue without confirming organ identity
options.clinical_reasoning: Leads to potential removal of incorrect tissue

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant removed benign pancreatic tissue instead of the adrenal gland

expected_action_court.description: Correct identification and removal of the adrenal gland should have been ensured
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established the standard of care

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to remove the correct organ breached the standard of care
explanation.what_should_have_happened: The adrenal gland containing the tumor should have been removed

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered from worsening abdominal pain and required treatment for injured pancreas
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Continued suffering from malignant adrenal tumor

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to remove the adrenal gland
Causation established - failure to remove tumor led to continued malignancy

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to remove the adrenal gland containing the tumor
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Correct removal of the adrenal gland would have addressed the malignancy
